As a point of comparison, when applying the debiased lasso on just the United States, there are three significant covariates: (i) the amount of students receiving free lunch with a p-value of 0.02230.02230.0223, (ii) the amount of print books with different titles with a p-value of 0.04180.04180.0418, and (iii) the principal’s report on how the school’s capacity to provide instruction was affected by resource shortage with a p-value of 0.01770.01770.0177. However, when using deconvlution, we see that the amount of students receiving free lunch is no longer significant (p-value of 0.05640.05640.0564), but both of the other two covariates continue to have a significant, positive effect (p-values of 0.02720.02720.0272 and 0.03550.03550.0355 respectively). Although our empirical Bayes confidence regions are strictly shorter than the classical confidence intervals, the regions are not necessarily contained in each other. For the amount of students receiving free lunch, both are in fact intervals and a 95%percent9595\% empirical Bayes confidence region is (−0.2646,18.9489)0.264618.9489(-0.2646,18.9489) while a classical confidence interval is (0.3749,19.8356)0.374919.8356(0.3749,19.8356).

On the other hand, for a new polity, we rank the covariates by the maximum of the estimated probabilities of having a positive effect and of having a negative effect. Then, the principal’s report on how problematic school fights are has the highest probability of having a negative impact on student performance, with an estimated probability greater than 75%percent7575\%. This suggests that reducing school fights is likely to improve student performance in a polity that did not participate in TIMSS.

8 Proofs

Here, we provide the proof of all of the results along with supplemental lemmata.

Proof of Proposition 1.

Indeed, consider the optimization problem

minimizeminimize\displaystyle\operatorname{minimize} 𝔼𝐔0,θ0​λ​(ℐ)subscript𝔼subscript𝐔0subscript𝜃0𝜆ℐ\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{U}_{0},\theta_{0}}\lambda(\mathcal{I})
subject​tosubjectto\displaystyle\operatorname{subject\hskip 3.00003ptto} pr𝐔0,θ0⁡(ℐ∋θ0)≥1−α.subscriptprsubscript𝐔0subscript𝜃0subscript𝜃0ℐ1𝛼\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\operatorname{pr}_{\mathbf{U}_{0},\theta_{0}}(\mathcal{I}\ni\theta_{0})\geq 1-\alpha.

Expanding the first term, we have

𝔼𝐔0,θ0​λ​(ℐ)subscript𝔼subscript𝐔0subscript𝜃0𝜆ℐ\displaystyle\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{U}_{0},\theta_{0}}\lambda(\mathcal{I}) =∫ℝu0f​(𝐔0)​λ​(d​𝐔0)​∫Θλ​(ℐ)​π​(θ0|𝐔0)​λ​(d​θ0)absentsubscriptsuperscriptℝsubscript𝑢0𝑓subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝐔0subscriptΘ𝜆ℐ𝜋conditionalsubscript𝜃0subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃0\displaystyle=\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}f(\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})\int_{\Theta}\lambda(\mathcal{I})\pi(\theta_{0}|\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})
=∫ℝu0f​(𝐔0)​λ​(d​𝐔0)​∫Θπ​(θ0|𝐔0)​λ​(d​θ0)​∫Θ𝟙​{ℐ∋ϑ0}​λ​(d​ϑ0).absentsubscriptsuperscriptℝsubscript𝑢0𝑓subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝐔0subscriptΘ𝜋conditionalsubscript𝜃0subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃0subscriptΘ1subscriptitalic-ϑ0ℐ𝜆𝑑subscriptitalic-ϑ0\displaystyle=\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}f(\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})\int_{\Theta}\pi(\theta_{0}|\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\Theta}\mathbbm{1}\left\{\mathcal{I}\ni\vartheta_{0}\right\}\lambda(d\vartheta_{0}).

Similarly, expanding the second term yields

pr𝐔0,θ0⁡(ℐ∋θ0)subscriptprsubscript𝐔0subscript𝜃0subscript𝜃0ℐ\displaystyle\operatorname{pr}_{\mathbf{U}_{0},\theta_{0}}(\mathcal{I}\ni\theta_{0}) =∫ℝu0f​(𝐔0)​λ​(d​𝐔0)​∫Θ𝟙​{ℐ∋θ0}​π​(θ0|𝐔0)​λ​(d​θ0)absentsubscriptsuperscriptℝsubscript𝑢0𝑓subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝐔0subscriptΘ1subscript𝜃0ℐ𝜋conditionalsubscript𝜃0subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃0\displaystyle=\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}f(\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})\int_{\Theta}\mathbbm{1}\left\{\mathcal{I}\ni\theta_{0}\right\}\pi(\theta_{0}|\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})
=∫ℝu0f​(𝐔0)​λ​(d​𝐔0)​∫Θ𝟙​{ℐ∋ϑ0}​π​(ϑ0|𝐔0)​λ​(d​ϑ0)absentsubscriptsuperscriptℝsubscript𝑢0𝑓subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝐔0subscriptΘ1subscriptitalic-ϑ0ℐ𝜋conditionalsubscriptitalic-ϑ0subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscriptitalic-ϑ0\displaystyle=\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}f(\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})\int_{\Theta}\mathbbm{1}\left\{\mathcal{I}\ni\vartheta_{0}\right\}\pi(\vartheta_{0}|\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\vartheta_{0})
=∫ℝu0f​(𝐔0)​λ​(d​𝐔0)​∫Θπ​(θ0|𝐔0)​λ​(d​θ0)​∫Θ𝟙​{ℐ∋ϑ0}​π​(ϑ0|𝐔0)​λ​(d​ϑ0)absentsubscriptsuperscriptℝsubscript𝑢0𝑓subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝐔0subscriptΘ𝜋conditionalsubscript𝜃0subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃0subscriptΘ1subscriptitalic-ϑ0ℐ𝜋conditionalsubscriptitalic-ϑ0subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscriptitalic-ϑ0\displaystyle=\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}f(\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})\int_{\Theta}\pi(\theta_{0}|\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\Theta}\mathbbm{1}\left\{\mathcal{I}\ni\vartheta_{0}\right\}\pi(\vartheta_{0}|\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\vartheta_{0})

where we change the variable of integration θ0↦ϑ0maps-tosubscript𝜃0subscriptitalic-ϑ0\theta_{0}\mapsto\vartheta_{0} in the second line and use the fact that π​(θ0|𝐔0)𝜋conditionalsubscript𝜃0subscript𝐔0\pi(\theta_{0}|\mathbf{U}_{0}) integrates to one in the last line. Now, letting γ>0𝛾0\gamma>0 be a Lagrange multiplier and combining the above calculations, we have

𝔼𝐔0,θ0​λ​(ℐ)−γ​pr𝐔0,θ0⁡(ℐ∋θ0)subscript𝔼subscript𝐔0subscript𝜃0𝜆ℐ𝛾subscriptprsubscript𝐔0subscript𝜃0subscript𝜃0ℐ\displaystyle\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{U}_{0},\theta_{0}}\lambda(\mathcal{I})-\gamma\operatorname{pr}_{\mathbf{U}_{0},\theta_{0}}(\mathcal{I}\ni\theta_{0})
=∫ℝu0f​(𝐔0)​λ​(d​𝐔0)​∫Θπ​(θ0|𝐔0)​λ​(d​θ0)​∫Θ𝟙​{ℐ∋ϑ0}​(1−γ​π​(ϑ0|𝐔0))​λ​(d​ϑ0).absentsubscriptsuperscriptℝsubscript𝑢0𝑓subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝐔0subscriptΘ𝜋conditionalsubscript𝜃0subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃0subscriptΘ1subscriptitalic-ϑ0ℐ1𝛾𝜋conditionalsubscriptitalic-ϑ0subscript𝐔0𝜆𝑑subscriptitalic-ϑ0\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}f(\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})\int_{\Theta}\pi(\theta_{0}|\mathbf{U}_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\Theta}\mathbbm{1}\left\{\mathcal{I}\ni\vartheta_{0}\right\}\Big{(}1-\gamma\pi(\vartheta_{0}|\mathbf{U}_{0})\Big{)}\lambda(d\vartheta_{0}).

From here, it is immediate that the above display is minimized when

ℐ={ϑ∈Θ:1−γ​π​(ϑ|𝐔0)<0}={ϑ∈Θ:π​(ϑ|𝐔0)>1γ}ℐconditional-setitalic-ϑΘ1𝛾𝜋conditionalitalic-ϑsubscript𝐔00conditional-setitalic-ϑΘ𝜋conditionalitalic-ϑsubscript𝐔01𝛾\displaystyle\mathcal{I}=\Big{\{}\vartheta\in\Theta:1-\gamma\pi(\vartheta|\mathbf{U}_{0})<0\Big{\}}=\Big{\{}\vartheta\in\Theta:\pi(\vartheta|\mathbf{U}_{0})>\frac{1}{\gamma}\Big{\}}

Finally, noting that γ=1/τ𝛾1𝜏\gamma=1/\tau is the smallest value satisfying the constraint by construction and λ​(ℐ)𝜆ℐ\lambda(\mathcal{I}) is monotonically increasing in γ𝛾\gamma finishes the proof. ∎

Proof of Proposition 2.

Indeed, we have

∫Θλ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)πn(θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)π^n(θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)\displaystyle\int_{\Theta}\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}\Big{|}f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{n}(\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\hat{\pi}_{n}(\theta_{0})\Big{|}\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})
≤∫Θλ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)πn(θ0)−fn(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq\int_{\Theta}\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}\Big{|}f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{n}(\theta_{0})-f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\Big{|}\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})
+∫Θλ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\phantom{\leq}+\int_{\Theta}\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}\Big{|}f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\Big{|}\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})
+∫Θλ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|f^n(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)πn(θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\phantom{\leq}+\int_{\Theta}\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}\Big{|}\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{n}(\theta_{0})\Big{|}\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})
+∫Θλ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|f^n(𝐔0|θ0)πn(θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)π^n(θ0)|λ(d𝐔0).\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\phantom{\leq}+\int_{\Theta}\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}\Big{|}\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{n}(\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\hat{\pi}_{n}(\theta_{0})\Big{|}\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0}).

We consider each of the four terms on the right hand side separately. First, note that

∫Θλ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)πn(θ0)−fn(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)\displaystyle\int_{\Theta}\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}\Big{|}f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{n}(\theta_{0})-f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\Big{|}\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})
=∫Θ|πn​(θ0)−π∞​(θ0)|​λ​(d​θ0)​∫ℝu0fn​(𝐔0|θ0)​λ​(d​𝐔0)absentsubscriptΘsubscript𝜋𝑛subscript𝜃0subscript𝜋subscript𝜃0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃0subscriptsuperscriptℝsubscript𝑢0subscript𝑓𝑛conditionalsubscript𝐔0subscript𝜃0𝜆𝑑subscript𝐔0\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\int_{\Theta}|\pi_{n}(\theta_{0})-\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})|\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})
=∫Θ|πn​(θ0)−π∞​(θ0)|​λ​(d​θ0)absentsubscriptΘsubscript𝜋𝑛subscript𝜃0subscript𝜋subscript𝜃0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃0\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\int_{\Theta}|\pi_{n}(\theta_{0})-\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})|\lambda(d\theta_{0})
=‖πn​(⋅)−π∞​(⋅)‖1.absentsubscriptnormsubscript𝜋𝑛⋅subscript𝜋⋅1\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\|\pi_{n}(\cdot)-\pi_{\infty}(\cdot)\|_{1}.

The above converges to zero as πnsubscript𝜋𝑛\pi_{n} converges to π∞subscript𝜋\pi_{\infty} in total variation distance. The proof for the third and fourth terms are analogous and, thus, omitted.

For the second term, let (Υj)j=1∞⊆ΘsuperscriptsubscriptsubscriptΥ𝑗𝑗1Θ(\Upsilon_{j})_{j=1}^{\infty}\subseteq\Theta be a collection of compact sets such that ∪j=1∞Υj=Θsuperscriptsubscript𝑗1subscriptΥ𝑗Θ\cup_{j=1}^{\infty}\Upsilon_{j}=\Theta and λ​(Υj∩Υj′)=0𝜆subscriptΥ𝑗subscriptΥsuperscript𝑗′0\lambda(\Upsilon_{j}\cap\Upsilon_{j^{\prime}})=0 for j≠j′𝑗superscript𝑗′j\neq j^{\prime}. Note that

∑j=1∞∫Υjπ​(θ0)​λ​(d​θ0)=∫Θπ​(θ0)​λ​(d​θ0)=1.superscriptsubscript𝑗1subscriptsubscriptΥ𝑗𝜋subscript𝜃0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃0subscriptΘ𝜋subscript𝜃0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃01\displaystyle\sum_{j=1}^{\infty}\int_{\Upsilon_{j}}\pi(\theta_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})=\int_{\Theta}\pi(\theta_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})=1.

Now, fix a value of ε>0𝜀0\varepsilon>0 and choose J𝐽J sufficiently large such that

∑j=J+1∞∫Υjπ​(θ0)​λ​(d​θ0)<ε/4.superscriptsubscript𝑗𝐽1subscriptsubscriptΥ𝑗𝜋subscript𝜃0𝜆𝑑subscript𝜃0𝜀4\displaystyle\sum_{j=J+1}^{\infty}\int_{\Upsilon_{j}}\pi(\theta_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})<\varepsilon/4.

Then,

∫Θλ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)\displaystyle\int_{\Theta}\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}\Big{|}f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\Big{|}\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})
≤∫Θπ∞(θ0)λ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq\int_{\Theta}\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}|f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})|\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})
=∑j=1J∫Υjπ∞(θ0)λ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\sum_{j=1}^{J}\int_{\Upsilon_{j}}\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}|f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})|\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})
+∑j=J+1∞∫Υjπ∞(θ0)λ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\phantom{\leq}+\sum_{j=J+1}^{\infty}\int_{\Upsilon_{j}}\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}|f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})|\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})

Letting N𝑁N be sufficiently large such that for all n≥N𝑛𝑁n\geq N

maxj=1,…,Jsupθ0∈Υj∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)<ε/2,\displaystyle\max_{j=1,\dots,J}\sup_{\theta_{0}\in\Upsilon_{j}}\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}|f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})|\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})<\varepsilon/2,

we have

∑j=1J∫Υjπ∞(θ0)λ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)<(1−ε)ε/2<ε/2.\displaystyle\sum_{j=1}^{J}\int_{\Upsilon_{j}}\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}|f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})|\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})<(1-\varepsilon)\varepsilon/2<\varepsilon/2.

Likewise, since

∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)≤2,\displaystyle\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}|f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})|\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})\leq 2,

it follows that

∑j=J+1∞∫Υjπ∞(θ0)λ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)<ε/2,\displaystyle\sum_{j=J+1}^{\infty}\int_{\Upsilon_{j}}\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}|f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})|\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})<\varepsilon/2,

implying further that

∫Θλ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)<ε.\displaystyle\int_{\Theta}\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}\Big{|}f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\Big{|}\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})<\varepsilon.

Since ε>0𝜀0\varepsilon>0 is arbitrary, we conclude that

lim supn→∞∫Θλ(dθ0)∫ℝu0|fn(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)−f^n(𝐔0|θ0)π∞(θ0)|λ(d𝐔0)=0.\displaystyle\limsup_{n\to\infty}\int_{\Theta}\lambda(d\theta_{0})\int_{\mathbb{R}^{u_{0}}}\Big{|}f_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})-\hat{f}_{n}(\mathbf{U}_{0}|\theta_{0})\pi_{\infty}(\theta_{0})\Big{|}\lambda(d\mathbf{U}_{0})=0.

Combining the above calculations finishes the proof. ∎

Proof of Corollary 2.1.

The result is an immediate consequence of Scheffé’s Theorem and Proposition 2. ∎

Proof of Proposition 3.

Indeed, let hn(⋅|θ(n))h_{n}(\cdot|\theta^{(n)}) denote the density of n​(θ^(n)−θ(n))|θ(n)conditional𝑛superscript^𝜃𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛\sqrt{n}(\hat{\theta}^{(n)}-\theta^{(n)})|\theta^{(n)}. Then, by a change of variables, it follows that

∥fn(⋅|θ(n))−φ(⋅|nθ(n),σ2)∥1=∥hn(⋅|θ(n))−φ(⋅|0,σ2)∥1.\displaystyle\|f_{n}(\cdot|\theta^{(n)})-\varphi(\cdot|\sqrt{n}\theta^{(n)},\sigma^{2})\|_{1}=\|h_{n}(\cdot|\theta^{(n)})-\varphi(\cdot|0,\sigma^{2})\|_{1}.

Furthermore, by construction, letting h~n(⋅|θ)\tilde{h}_{n}(\cdot|\theta) denote the density of n​(θ~(n)−θ(n))|θ(n)conditional𝑛superscript~𝜃𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛\sqrt{n}(\tilde{\theta}^{(n)}-\theta^{(n)})|\theta^{(n)}, we have

hn=h~n∗φ0,ςn2.subscriptℎ𝑛∗subscript~ℎ𝑛subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛\displaystyle h_{n}=\tilde{h}_{n}\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}.

Thus, the triangle inequality yields

∥hn(⋅|θ(n))−φ(⋅|0,σ2)∥1≤∥(h~n−φ0,σ2)∗φ0,ςn2∥1+∥φ0,σ2∗φ0,ςn2−φ0,σ2∥1.\displaystyle\|h_{n}(\cdot|\theta^{(n)})-\varphi(\cdot|0,\sigma^{2})\|_{1}\leq\|(\tilde{h}_{n}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{1}+\|\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{1}.

Note that φ0,ςn2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}} is an approximate convolutional identity as ςn2→0→subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛0\varsigma^{2}_{n}\to 0, implying that

‖φ0,σ2∗φ0,ςn2−φ0,σ2‖1=o​(1)subscriptnorm∗subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎21𝑜1\displaystyle\|\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{1}=o(1)

(for example, see Theorem 4.2.4 of ? (?)). As the above does not depend on θ∈Θ𝜃Θ\theta\in\Theta, the convergence is uniform over compacts Υ⊆ΘΥΘ\Upsilon\subseteq\Theta; that is,

supθ∈Υ‖φ0,σ2∗φ0,ςn2−φ0,σ2‖1=o​(1).subscriptsupremum𝜃Υsubscriptnorm∗subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎21𝑜1\displaystyle\sup_{\theta\in\Upsilon}\|\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{1}=o(1).

Now, for the other term, we have

‖(h~n−φ0,σ2)∗φ0,ςn2‖∞=‖(H~n−Φ0,σ2)∗φ0,ςn2′‖∞≤‖H~n−Φ0,σ2‖∞​‖φ0,ςn2′‖1,subscriptnorm∗subscript~ℎ𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛subscriptnorm∗subscript~𝐻𝑛subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2superscriptsubscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛′subscriptnormsubscript~𝐻𝑛subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2subscriptnormsuperscriptsubscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛′1\displaystyle\|(\tilde{h}_{n}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{\infty}=\|(\tilde{H}_{n}-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}})\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}^{\prime}\|_{\infty}\leq\|\tilde{H}_{n}-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{\infty}\|\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}^{\prime}\|_{1},

where H~n(⋅|θ)\tilde{H}_{n}(\cdot|\theta) denotes the distribution function corresponding to h~n(⋅|θ)\tilde{h}_{n}(\cdot|\theta). The inequality in the above display is a consequence of Young’s convolution inequality. Let {ϵn}n=1∞superscriptsubscriptsubscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛𝑛1\{\epsilon_{n}\}_{n=1}^{\infty} be a positive sequences of numbers decreasing to zero such that

pr⁡(|n​δn|≥ϵn)≤ϵn.pr𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|\geq\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}\leq\epsilon_{n}.

Such a sequence ϵnsubscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\epsilon_{n} exists as δn=opr​(n−1/2)subscript𝛿𝑛subscript𝑜prsuperscript𝑛12\delta_{n}=o_{\operatorname{pr}}(n^{-1/2}). Then, for any t∈ℝ𝑡ℝt\in\mathbb{R}, we have

H~n​(t)=pr⁡(n​(θ~(n)−θ(n))+δn≤t)=pr⁡(1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)+n​δn≤t).subscript~𝐻𝑛𝑡pr𝑛superscript~𝜃𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛𝑡pr1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛𝑡\displaystyle\tilde{H}_{n}(t)=\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\sqrt{n}(\tilde{\theta}^{(n)}-\theta^{(n)})+\delta_{n}\leq t\Big{)}=\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)+\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}\leq t\Big{)}.

Note that

{1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t−ϵn}∩{|n​δn|≤ϵn}⊆{1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)+n​δn≤t}1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛𝑡\displaystyle\Big{\{}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t-\epsilon_{n}\Big{\}}\cap\Big{\{}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|\leq\epsilon_{n}\Big{\}}\subseteq\Big{\{}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)+\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}\leq t\Big{\}}

and

{1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)+n​δn≤t}⊆{1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t+ϵn}∪{|n​δn|>ϵn},1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛𝑡1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\Big{\{}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)+\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}\leq t\Big{\}}\subseteq\Big{\{}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t+\epsilon_{n}\Big{\}}\cup\Big{\{}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|>\epsilon_{n}\Big{\}},

which implies

pr⁡(1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t−ϵn)+pr⁡(|n​δn|≤ϵn)−1pr1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛pr𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛1\displaystyle\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t-\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}+\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|\leq\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}-1
≤pr⁡(1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)+n​δn≤t)absentpr1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛𝑡\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)+\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}\leq t\Big{)}
≤pr⁡(1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t+ϵn)+pr⁡(|n​δn|>ϵn).absentpr1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛pr𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\phantom{\leq}\leq\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t+\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}+\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|>\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}.

Therefore,

‖H~n−Φ0,σ2‖∞subscriptnormsubscript~𝐻𝑛subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2\displaystyle\|\tilde{H}_{n}-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{\infty}
=supt∈ℝ|pr⁡(1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)+n​δn≤t)−Φ0,σ2​(t)|absentsubscriptsupremum𝑡ℝpr1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛𝑡subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)+\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}\leq t\Big{)}-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t)\Big{|}
≤max{supt∈ℝ|pr(1n∑i=1nψ(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t+ϵn)+pr(|nδn|>ϵn)−Φ0,σ2(t)|,\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq\max\Big{\{}\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t+\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}+\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|>\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t)\Big{|},
supt∈ℝ|pr(1n∑i=1nψ(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t−ϵn)+pr(|nδn|≤ϵn)−1−Φ0,σ2(t)|}.\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\phantom{\leq\max\Big{\{}\leq}\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t-\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}+\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|\leq\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}-1-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t)\Big{|}\Big{\}}.

Now, by the mean value theorem, we see that, for any s∈ℝ𝑠ℝs\in\mathbb{R},

|Φ0,σ2​(t)−Φ0,σ2​(s)|≤|t−s|2​π​σ2.subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑠𝑡𝑠2𝜋superscript𝜎2\displaystyle|\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t)-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(s)|\leq\frac{|t-s|}{\sqrt{2\pi\sigma^{2}}}.

Thus, it follows from the Berry-Esseen theorem that

supt∈ℝ|pr⁡(1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t+ϵn)+pr⁡(|n​δn|>ϵn)−Φ0,σ2​(t)|subscriptsupremum𝑡ℝpr1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛pr𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡\displaystyle\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t+\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}+\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|>\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t)\Big{|}
≤supt∈ℝ|pr⁡(1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t+ϵn)−Φ0,σ2​(t+ϵn)|absentsubscriptsupremum𝑡ℝpr1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t+\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t+\epsilon_{n})\Big{|}
+supt∈ℝ|Φ0,σ2​(t+ϵn)−Φ0,σ2​(t)|+supt∈ℝpr⁡(|n​δn|>ϵn)subscriptsupremum𝑡ℝsubscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡subscriptsupremum𝑡ℝpr𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\phantom{\leq}+\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t+\epsilon_{n})-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t)\Big{|}+\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|>\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}
=𝒪​(1n+ϵn).absent𝒪1𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\mathcal{O}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}+\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}.

Similarly, we have

supt∈ℝ|pr⁡(1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t−ϵn)+pr⁡(|n​δn|≤ϵn)−1−Φ0,σ2​(t)|subscriptsupremum𝑡ℝpr1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛pr𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛1subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡\displaystyle\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t-\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}+\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|\leq\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}-1-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t)\Big{|}
≤supt∈ℝ|pr⁡(1n​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)≤t−ϵn)−Φ0,σ2​(t−ϵn)|absentsubscriptsupremum𝑡ℝpr1𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)\leq t-\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t-\epsilon_{n})\Big{|}
+supt∈ℝ|Φ0,σ2​(t−ϵn)−Φ0,σ2​(t)|+supt∈ℝpr⁡(|n​δn|>ϵn)subscriptsupremum𝑡ℝsubscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝑡subscriptsupremum𝑡ℝpr𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\phantom{\leq}+\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t-\epsilon_{n})-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}(t)\Big{|}+\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\operatorname{pr}\Big{(}|\sqrt{n}\delta_{n}|>\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}
=𝒪​(1n+ϵn).absent𝒪1𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\mathcal{O}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}+\epsilon_{n}\Big{)}.

From Assumption B, since the third moment is uniformly bounded on compacts, the convergence is uniform. Hence, we conclude that

supθ∈Υ‖H~n−Φ0,σ2‖∞=𝒪​(1n+ϵn)subscriptsupremum𝜃Υsubscriptnormsubscript~𝐻𝑛subscriptΦ0superscript𝜎2𝒪1𝑛subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑛\displaystyle\sup_{\theta\in\Upsilon}\|\tilde{H}_{n}-\Phi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{\infty}=\mathcal{O}\Big{(}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}+\epsilon_{n}\Big{)} (6)

for all compacts Υ⊆ΘΥΘ\Upsilon\subseteq\Theta. Moreover, a direct calculation shows that

‖φ0,ςn2′‖1=∫−∞∞|ϑ|ςn2​2​π​ςn2​exp⁡(−ϑ22​ςn2)​λ​(d​ϑ)=1ςn​2π.subscriptnormsuperscriptsubscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛′1superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϑsubscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛2𝜋subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛superscriptitalic-ϑ22subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝜆𝑑italic-ϑ1subscript𝜍𝑛2𝜋\displaystyle\|\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}^{\prime}\|_{1}=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\frac{|\vartheta|}{\varsigma^{2}_{n}\sqrt{2\pi\varsigma^{2}_{n}}}\exp\Big{(}-\frac{\vartheta^{2}}{2\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\Big{)}\lambda(d\vartheta)=\frac{1}{\varsigma_{n}}\sqrt{\frac{2}{\pi}}.

Combining the above calculations yields

supθ∈Υ‖(h~n−φ0,σ2)∗φ0,ςn2‖∞=o​(1)subscriptsupremum𝜃Υsubscriptnorm∗subscript~ℎ𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑜1\displaystyle\sup_{\theta\in\Upsilon}\|(\tilde{h}_{n}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{\infty}=o(1)

for all compacts Υ⊆ΘΥΘ\Upsilon\subseteq\Theta. Writing h~n,θ(n)(⋅)=h~n(⋅|θ(n))\tilde{h}_{n,\theta^{(n)}}(\cdot)=\tilde{h}_{n}(\cdot|\theta^{(n)}), the above implies that {h~n,θ(n)∗φ0,ςn2}n∈ℕ,θ∈Υsubscript∗subscript~ℎ𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛formulae-sequence𝑛ℕ𝜃Υ\{\tilde{h}_{n,\theta^{(n)}}\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\}_{n\in\mathbb{N},\theta\in\Upsilon} is a tight family of probability measures for all compacts Υ⊆ΘΥΘ\Upsilon\subseteq\Theta. It is left to show that

supθ∈Υ‖(h~n−φ0,σ2)∗φ0,ςn2‖1=o​(1).subscriptsupremum𝜃Υsubscriptnorm∗subscript~ℎ𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛1𝑜1\displaystyle\sup_{\theta\in\Upsilon}\|(\tilde{h}_{n}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{1}=o(1).

Thus, for an arbitrary value of ε>0𝜀0\varepsilon>0, let Ωε⊆ℝsubscriptΩ𝜀ℝ\Omega_{\varepsilon}\subseteq\mathbb{R} be a compact set such that

infn{infθ∈Υ∫Ωε𝖢(h~n,θ(n)∗φ0,ςn2)​(t)​λ​(d​t)+∫Ωε𝖢φ0,σ2+ςn2​(t)​λ​(d​t)}<ε/2.subscriptinfimum𝑛subscriptinfimum𝜃ΥsubscriptsuperscriptsubscriptΩ𝜀𝖢∗subscript~ℎ𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡subscriptsuperscriptsubscriptΩ𝜀𝖢subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡𝜀2\displaystyle\inf_{n}\Big{\{}\inf_{\theta\in\Upsilon}\int_{\Omega_{\varepsilon}^{\mathsf{C}}}(\tilde{h}_{n,\theta^{(n)}}\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}})(t)\lambda(dt)+\int_{\Omega_{\varepsilon}^{\mathsf{C}}}\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}(t)\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}<\varepsilon/2.

Set N𝑁N sufficiently large such that for all n≥N𝑛𝑁n\geq N

supθ∈Υ‖(h~n−φ0,σ2)∗φ0,ςn2‖∞<ε/(2​λ​(Ωε)).subscriptsupremum𝜃Υsubscriptnorm∗subscript~ℎ𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝜀2𝜆subscriptΩ𝜀\displaystyle\sup_{\theta\in\Upsilon}\|(\tilde{h}_{n}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{\infty}<\varepsilon/(2\lambda(\Omega_{\varepsilon})).

Then, we have

supθ∈Υ‖(h~n−φ0,σ2)∗φ0,ςn2‖1subscriptsupremum𝜃Υsubscriptnorm∗subscript~ℎ𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛1\displaystyle\sup_{\theta\in\Upsilon}\|(\tilde{h}_{n}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{1} =supθ∈Υ∫Ωε|[(h~n−φ0,σ2)∗φ0,ςn2]​(t)|​λ​(d​t)+supθ∈Υ∫Ωε𝖢|[(h~n−φ0,σ2)∗φ0,ςn2]​(t)|​λ​(d​t)absentsubscriptsupremum𝜃ΥsubscriptsubscriptΩ𝜀delimited-[]∗subscript~ℎ𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡subscriptsupremum𝜃ΥsubscriptsuperscriptsubscriptΩ𝜀𝖢delimited-[]∗subscript~ℎ𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡\displaystyle=\sup_{\theta\in\Upsilon}\int_{\Omega_{\varepsilon}}|[(\tilde{h}_{n}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}](t)|\lambda(dt)+\sup_{\theta\in\Upsilon}\int_{\Omega_{\varepsilon}^{\mathsf{C}}}|[(\tilde{h}_{n}-\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})\ast\varphi_{0,\varsigma^{2}_{n}}](t)|\lambda(dt)
≤ε.absent𝜀\displaystyle\leq\varepsilon.

Combining the above calculations finishes the proof. ∎

Lemma 1.

For μ1,μ2∈ℝsubscript𝜇1subscript𝜇2ℝ\mu_{1},\mu_{2}\in\mathbb{R} and σ12,σ22>0superscriptsubscript𝜎12superscriptsubscript𝜎220\sigma_{1}^{2},\sigma_{2}^{2}>0, the following bound holds:

‖φμ1,σ12−φμ2,σ22‖12≤12​log⁡σ22σ12+σ12+(μ1−μ2)22​σ22−12.superscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝜑subscript𝜇1superscriptsubscript𝜎12subscript𝜑subscript𝜇2superscriptsubscript𝜎221212superscriptsubscript𝜎22superscriptsubscript𝜎12superscriptsubscript𝜎12superscriptsubscript𝜇1subscript𝜇222superscriptsubscript𝜎2212\displaystyle\|\varphi_{\mu_{1},\sigma_{1}^{2}}-\varphi_{\mu_{2},\sigma_{2}^{2}}\|_{1}^{2}\leq\frac{1}{2}\log\frac{\sigma_{2}^{2}}{\sigma_{1}^{2}}+\frac{\sigma_{1}^{2}+(\mu_{1}-\mu_{2})^{2}}{2\sigma_{2}^{2}}-\frac{1}{2}.
Proof of Lemma 1.

The result follows from Pinsker’s inequality. ∎

Proof of Corollary 3.1.

From Proposition 3, it suffices to show that

‖φn​θ(n),σ2−φn​θ(n),σ^2‖1=opr​(1).subscriptnormsubscript𝜑𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛superscript𝜎2subscript𝜑𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛superscript^𝜎21subscript𝑜pr1\displaystyle\|\varphi_{\sqrt{n}\theta^{(n)},\sigma^{2}}-\varphi_{\sqrt{n}\theta^{(n)},\hat{\sigma}^{2}}\|_{1}=o_{\operatorname{pr}}(1).

The result now follows from Lemma 1. ∎

Lemma 2.

Let 1≤q<p≤∞1𝑞𝑝1\leq q<p\leq\infty. If {gn}n=1∞superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑛1\{g_{n}\}_{n=1}^{\infty} and g​(⋅)𝑔⋅g(\cdot) are probability densities such that ‖gn−g‖p→0→subscriptnormsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑔𝑝0\|g_{n}-g\|_{p}\to 0, then ‖gn−g‖q→0→subscriptnormsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑔𝑞0\|g_{n}-g\|_{q}\to 0.

Proof of Lemma 2.

We start by showing the result holds if 1=q<p≤∞1𝑞𝑝1=q<p\leq\infty. It suffices to show that each subsequence {nk}k=1∞superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑛𝑘𝑘1\{n_{k}\}_{k=1}^{\infty} contains a further subsequence {nkl}l=1∞superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑛subscript𝑘𝑙𝑙1\{n_{k_{l}}\}_{l=1}^{\infty} such that ‖gnkl−g‖1→0→subscriptnormsubscript𝑔subscript𝑛subscript𝑘𝑙𝑔10\|g_{n_{k_{l}}}-g\|_{1}\to 0. To this end, let {nk}k=1∞superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑛𝑘𝑘1\{n_{k}\}_{k=1}^{\infty} be an arbitrary subsequence. Then, since ‖gnk−g‖p→0→subscriptnormsubscript𝑔subscript𝑛𝑘𝑔𝑝0\|g_{n_{k}}-g\|_{p}\to 0, there exists a further subsequence {nkl}l=1∞superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑛subscript𝑘𝑙𝑙1\{n_{k_{l}}\}_{l=1}^{\infty} such that gnkl→g→subscript𝑔subscript𝑛subscript𝑘𝑙𝑔g_{n_{k_{l}}}\to g almost everywhere. Thus, by Scheffé’s lemma, it follows that ‖gnkl−g‖1→0→subscriptnormsubscript𝑔subscript𝑛subscript𝑘𝑙𝑔10\|g_{n_{k_{l}}}-g\|_{1}\to 0.

Now, suppose instead that q>1𝑞1q>1. Expanding out the integral, we have

‖gn−g‖qqsuperscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑔𝑞𝑞\displaystyle\|g_{n}-g\|_{q}^{q} =∫−∞∞|gn​(x)−g​(x)|q​λ​(d​x)absentsuperscriptsubscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥𝑞𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|^{q}\lambda(dx)
=∫|gn​(x)−g​(x)|>1|gn​(x)−g​(x)|q​λ​(d​x)+∫|gn​(x)−g​(x)|<1|gn​(x)−g​(x)|q​λ​(d​x).absentsubscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥1superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥𝑞𝜆𝑑𝑥subscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥1superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥𝑞𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle=\int_{|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|>1}|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|^{q}\lambda(dx)+\int_{|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|<1}|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|^{q}\lambda(dx).

If p<∞𝑝p<\infty, then

‖gn−g‖qqsuperscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑔𝑞𝑞\displaystyle\|g_{n}-g\|_{q}^{q} ≤∫|gn​(x)−g​(x)|>1|gn​(x)−g​(x)|p​λ​(d​x)+∫|gn​(x)−g​(x)|<1|gn​(x)−g​(x)|​λ​(d​x)absentsubscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥1superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥𝑝𝜆𝑑𝑥subscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥1subscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle\leq\int_{|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|>1}|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|^{p}\lambda(dx)+\int_{|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|<1}|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|\lambda(dx)
≤‖gn−g‖pp+‖gn−g‖1absentsuperscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑔𝑝𝑝subscriptnormsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑔1\displaystyle\leq\|g_{n}-g\|_{p}^{p}+\|g_{n}-g\|_{1}
=o​(1)absent𝑜1\displaystyle=o(1)

by the previous case. If p=∞𝑝p=\infty, there exists an N𝑁N sufficiently large such that ‖gn−g‖∞<1subscriptnormsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑔1\|g_{n}-g\|_{\infty}<1 for all n≥N𝑛𝑁n\geq N. Thus,

∫|gn​(x)−g​(x)|>1|gn​(x)−g​(x)|q​λ​(d​x)=0subscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥1superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑛𝑥𝑔𝑥𝑞𝜆𝑑𝑥0\displaystyle\int_{|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|>1}|g_{n}(x)-g(x)|^{q}\lambda(dx)=0

for all n≥N𝑛𝑁n\geq N. This finishes the proof. ∎

Proof of Proposition 4.

Without a loss of generality, by recentering our distribution, we assume that 𝔼​θ(n)=0𝔼superscript𝜃𝑛0\mathbb{E}\theta^{(n)}=0 for all n𝑛n. Expanding the definition, we see that

an​θ^(n)=an​θ~(n)+an​ξnn=an​θ(n)+ann​∑i=1nψ​(Xi;θ(n),η)+an​δn+an​ξnn.subscript𝑎𝑛superscript^𝜃𝑛subscript𝑎𝑛superscript~𝜃𝑛subscript𝑎𝑛subscript𝜉𝑛𝑛subscript𝑎𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛subscript𝑎𝑛𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑛𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖superscript𝜃𝑛𝜂subscript𝑎𝑛subscript𝛿𝑛subscript𝑎𝑛subscript𝜉𝑛𝑛\displaystyle a_{n}\hat{\theta}^{(n)}=a_{n}\tilde{\theta}^{(n)}+\frac{a_{n}\xi_{n}}{n}=a_{n}\theta^{(n)}+\frac{a_{n}}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\psi(X_{i};\theta^{(n)},\eta)+a_{n}\delta_{n}+\frac{a_{n}\xi_{n}}{n}.

We start by considering the first assertion. If an=o​(n)subscript𝑎𝑛𝑜𝑛a_{n}=o(\sqrt{n}), then

an​θ^(n)=an​θ(n)+opr​(1)+an​ξnn.subscript𝑎𝑛superscript^𝜃𝑛subscript𝑎𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛subscript𝑜pr1subscript𝑎𝑛subscript𝜉𝑛𝑛\displaystyle a_{n}\hat{\theta}^{(n)}=a_{n}\theta^{(n)}+o_{\operatorname{pr}}(1)+\frac{a_{n}\xi_{n}}{n}.

By the triangle inequality, we have that

‖qn−ν∞‖2≤‖qn​(t)−ν∞∗φ0,an2​ςn2/n‖2+‖ν∞∗φ0,an2​ςn2/n−ν∞‖2.subscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛subscript𝜈2subscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛𝑡∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛2subscriptnorm∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛subscript𝜈2\displaystyle\|q_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\|_{2}\leq\|q_{n}(t)-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n}\|_{2}+\|\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n}-\nu_{\infty}\|_{2}.

If an2​ςn2/n→0→superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛0a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n\to 0, it follows by Theorem 4.2.4 of ? (?) that

‖ν∞∗φ0,an2​ςn2/n−ν∞‖2=o​(1).subscriptnorm∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛subscript𝜈2𝑜1\displaystyle\|\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n}-\nu_{\infty}\|_{2}=o(1).

Let Q~n​(⋅)subscript~𝑄𝑛⋅\tilde{Q}_{n}(\cdot) and q~n​(⋅)subscript~𝑞𝑛⋅\tilde{q}_{n}(\cdot) denote the marginal distribution function and marginal density of an​θ~(n)subscript𝑎𝑛superscript~𝜃𝑛a_{n}\tilde{\theta}^{(n)} respectively. Now, Young’s convolution inequality yields

‖qn−ν∞∗φ0,an2​ςn2/n‖∞=‖(q~n−ν∞)∗φ0,an2​ςn2/n‖∞=‖(Q~n−N∞)∗φ0,an2​ςn2/n′‖∞≤‖Q~n−N∞‖∞​‖φ0,an2​ςn2/n′‖1.subscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛subscriptnorm∗subscript~𝑞𝑛subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛subscriptnorm∗subscript~𝑄𝑛subscript𝑁superscriptsubscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛′subscriptnormsubscript~𝑄𝑛subscript𝑁subscriptnormsuperscriptsubscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛′1\displaystyle\|q_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n}\|_{\infty}=\|(\tilde{q}_{n}-\nu_{\infty})\ast\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n}\|_{\infty}=\|(\tilde{Q}_{n}-N_{\infty})\ast\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n}^{\prime}\|_{\infty}\leq\|\tilde{Q}_{n}-N_{\infty}\|_{\infty}\|\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n}^{\prime}\|_{1}.

Since N∞subscript𝑁N_{\infty} is a continuous distribution, the convergence of Q~nsubscript~𝑄𝑛\tilde{Q}_{n} to N∞subscript𝑁N_{\infty} is uniform (for example, see Exercise 8.1.13 of ? (?)), implying that ‖Q~n−N∞‖∞=o​(1)subscriptnormsubscript~𝑄𝑛subscript𝑁𝑜1\|\tilde{Q}_{n}-N_{\infty}\|_{\infty}=o(1). Moreover, since

‖φ0,an2​ςn2/n′‖1=2​nπ​an2​ςn2,subscriptnormsuperscriptsubscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛′12𝑛𝜋superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛\displaystyle\|\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n}^{\prime}\|_{1}=\sqrt{\frac{2n}{\pi a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}}},

there exists a sequence {ςn2}n=1∞superscriptsubscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛1\{\varsigma^{2}_{n}\}_{n=1}^{\infty} such that (i) an2​ςn2/n→0→superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛0a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n\to 0 and (ii)

‖Q~n−N∞‖∞​‖φ0,an2​ςn2/n′‖1=o​(1).subscriptnormsubscript~𝑄𝑛subscript𝑁subscriptnormsuperscriptsubscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑛′1𝑜1\displaystyle\|\tilde{Q}_{n}-N_{\infty}\|_{\infty}\|\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}\varsigma^{2}_{n}/n}^{\prime}\|_{1}=o(1).

With this choice of ςn2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛\varsigma^{2}_{n}, the first claim follows by Lemma 2.

Next, suppose instead that an=nsubscript𝑎𝑛𝑛a_{n}=\sqrt{n}. Again, we have the decomposition

‖qn−ν∞∗φ0,σ2‖2subscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎22\displaystyle\|q_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{2} ≤‖qn−νn∗φ0,σ2+ςn2‖2+‖νn∗φ0,σ2+ςn2−ν∞∗φ0,σ2+ςn2‖2absentsubscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛∗subscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛2subscriptnorm∗subscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛2\displaystyle\leq\|q_{n}-\nu_{n}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{2}+\|\nu_{n}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{2}
+‖ν∞∗φ0,σ2+ςn2−ν∞∗φ0,σ2‖2.subscriptnorm∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎22\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}+\|\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{2}.

Theorem 4.5.4 of ? (?) immediately implies ‖ν∞∗φ0,σ2+ςn2−ν∞∗φ0,σ2‖2=o​(1)subscriptnorm∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎22𝑜1\|\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{2}=o(1). Moreover, by Assumption C, it follows from Young’s convolution inequality that

‖νn∗φ0,σ2+ςn2−ν∞∗φ0,σ2+ςn2‖2≤‖νn−ν∞‖2​‖φ0,σ2+ςn2‖1=‖νn−ν∞‖2=o​(1).subscriptnorm∗subscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛2subscriptnormsubscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜈2subscriptnormsubscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛1subscriptnormsubscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜈2𝑜1\displaystyle\|\nu_{n}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{2}\leq\|\nu_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\|_{2}\|\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{1}=\|\nu_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\|_{2}=o(1).

It is left to show that ‖qn−νn∗φ0,σ2+ςn2‖2=o​(1)subscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛∗subscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛2𝑜1\|q_{n}-\nu_{n}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{2}=o(1). Following the proof of Proposition 3, let hn(⋅|θ(n))h_{n}(\cdot|\theta^{(n)}) and h~n(⋅|θ(n))\tilde{h}_{n}(\cdot|\theta^{(n)}) denote the distribution of n​(θ^(n)−θ(n))|θ(n)conditional𝑛superscript^𝜃𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛\sqrt{n}(\hat{\theta}^{(n)}-\theta^{(n)})|\theta^{(n)} and n​(θ~(n)−θ(n))|θ(n)conditional𝑛superscript~𝜃𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛superscript𝜃𝑛\sqrt{n}(\tilde{\theta}^{(n)}-\theta^{(n)})|\theta^{(n)} respectively. Then, a few applications of change of variables yields

qn​(t)subscript𝑞𝑛𝑡\displaystyle q_{n}(t) =n​∫−∞∞hn​(t−n​θ|θ)​πn​(θ)​λ​(d​θ)absent𝑛superscriptsubscriptsubscriptℎ𝑛𝑡conditional𝑛𝜃𝜃subscript𝜋𝑛𝜃𝜆𝑑𝜃\displaystyle=\sqrt{n}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}h_{n}(t-\sqrt{n}\theta|\theta)\pi_{n}(\theta)\lambda(d\theta)
=n​∫−∞∞hn​(t−n​θ|θ)​νn​(n​θ)​λ​(d​θ)absent𝑛superscriptsubscriptsubscriptℎ𝑛𝑡conditional𝑛𝜃𝜃subscript𝜈𝑛𝑛𝜃𝜆𝑑𝜃\displaystyle=\sqrt{n}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}h_{n}(t-\sqrt{n}\theta|\theta)\nu_{n}(\sqrt{n}\theta)\lambda(d\theta)
=n​∫−∞∞νn​(n​θ)​λ​(d​θ)​∫−∞∞h~n​(s|θ)​φ​(t−n​θ−s|0,ςn2)​λ​(d​s)absent𝑛superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝜈𝑛𝑛𝜃𝜆𝑑𝜃superscriptsubscriptsubscript~ℎ𝑛conditional𝑠𝜃𝜑𝑡𝑛𝜃conditional𝑠0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝜆𝑑𝑠\displaystyle=\sqrt{n}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\nu_{n}(\sqrt{n}\theta)\lambda(d\theta)\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\tilde{h}_{n}(s|\theta)\varphi(t-\sqrt{n}\theta-s|0,\varsigma^{2}_{n})\lambda(ds)

and

(νn∗φ0,σ2+ςn2)​(t)∗subscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑡\displaystyle(\nu_{n}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}})(t) =∫−∞∞νn​(θ)​φ​(t−θ|0,σ2+ςn2)​λ​(d​θ)absentsuperscriptsubscriptsubscript𝜈𝑛𝜃𝜑𝑡conditional𝜃0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝜆𝑑𝜃\displaystyle=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\nu_{n}(\theta)\varphi(t-\theta|0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n})\lambda(d\theta)
=n​∫−∞∞νn​(n​θ)​φ​(t−n​θ|0,σ2+ςn2)​λ​(d​θ)absent𝑛superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝜈𝑛𝑛𝜃𝜑𝑡conditional𝑛𝜃0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝜆𝑑𝜃\displaystyle=\sqrt{n}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\nu_{n}(\sqrt{n}\theta)\varphi(t-\sqrt{n}\theta|0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n})\lambda(d\theta)
=n​∫−∞∞νn​(n​θ)​λ​(d​θ)​∫−∞∞φ​(s|0,σ2)​φ​(t−n​θ−s|0,ςn2)​λ​(d​s).absent𝑛superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝜈𝑛𝑛𝜃𝜆𝑑𝜃superscriptsubscript𝜑conditional𝑠0superscript𝜎2𝜑𝑡𝑛𝜃conditional𝑠0subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝜆𝑑𝑠\displaystyle=\sqrt{n}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\nu_{n}(\sqrt{n}\theta)\lambda(d\theta)\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\varphi(s|0,\sigma^{2})\varphi(t-\sqrt{n}\theta-s|0,\varsigma^{2}_{n})\lambda(ds).

Thus, combining the above terms, we have

‖qn−νn∗φ0,σ2+ςn2)‖∞\displaystyle\|q_{n}-\nu_{n}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n})}\|_{\infty}
=supt∈ℝ|∫−∞∞nνn(nθ)λ(dθ)∫−∞∞[g~n(s|θ)−φ(s|0,σ2)]φ(t−nθ−s|0,ςn2)λ(ds)|\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\sqrt{n}\nu_{n}(\sqrt{n}\theta)\lambda(d\theta)\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}[\tilde{g}_{n}(s|\theta)-\varphi(s|0,\sigma^{2})]\varphi(t-\sqrt{n}\theta-s|0,\varsigma^{2}_{n})\lambda(ds)\Big{|}
=supt∈ℝ|∫−∞∞nνn(nθ)λ(dθ)∫−∞∞[G~n(s|θ)−Φ(s|0,σ2)]φ′(t−nθ−s|0,ςn2)λ(ds)|\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\sqrt{n}\nu_{n}(\sqrt{n}\theta)\lambda(d\theta)\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}[\tilde{G}_{n}(s|\theta)-\Phi(s|0,\sigma^{2})]\varphi^{\prime}(t-\sqrt{n}\theta-s|0,\varsigma^{2}_{n})\lambda(ds)\Big{|}
≤supt∈ℝ∫−∞∞nνn(nθ){sups∈ℝ|G~n(s|θ)−Φ(s|0,σ2)|}λ(dθ)∫−∞∞φ′(t−nθ−s|0,ςn2)λ(ds)\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq\sup_{t\in\mathbb{R}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\sqrt{n}\nu_{n}(\sqrt{n}\theta)\Big{\{}\sup_{s\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\tilde{G}_{n}(s|\theta)-\Phi(s|0,\sigma^{2})\Big{|}\Big{\}}\lambda(d\theta)\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\varphi^{\prime}(t-\sqrt{n}\theta-s|0,\varsigma^{2}_{n})\lambda(ds)
=2π​ςn2∫−∞∞nνn(nθ){sups∈ℝ|G~n(s|θ)−Φ(s|0,σ2)|}λ(dθ)\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\sqrt{\frac{2}{\pi\varsigma^{2}_{n}}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\sqrt{n}\nu_{n}(\sqrt{n}\theta)\Big{\{}\sup_{s\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\tilde{G}_{n}(s|\theta)-\Phi(s|0,\sigma^{2})\Big{|}\Big{\}}\lambda(d\theta)
=2π​ςn2∫−∞∞νn(θ){sups∈ℝ|G~n(s|θ/n)−Φ(s|0,σ2)|}λ(dθ).\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\sqrt{\frac{2}{\pi\varsigma^{2}_{n}}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\nu_{n}(\theta)\Big{\{}\sup_{s\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\tilde{G}_{n}(s|\theta/\sqrt{n})-\Phi(s|0,\sigma^{2})\Big{|}\Big{\}}\lambda(d\theta).

Since νnsubscript𝜈𝑛\nu_{n} converges in total variation to ν∞subscript𝜈\nu_{\infty}, the sequence of measures is tight. Hence, for ε>0𝜀0\varepsilon>0, there exists a compact set ΩεsubscriptΩ𝜀\Omega_{\varepsilon} such that

∫Ωενn​(θ)​λ​(d​θ)<ε/4.subscriptsubscriptΩ𝜀subscript𝜈𝑛𝜃𝜆𝑑𝜃𝜀4\displaystyle\int_{\Omega_{\varepsilon}}\nu_{n}(\theta)\lambda(d\theta)<\varepsilon/4.

Moreover, equation (6) implies that there exists an N𝑁N sufficiently large such that for all n≥N𝑛𝑁n\geq N

supθ∈Ωεsups∈ℝ|G~n(s|θ/n)−Φ(s|0,σ2)|<ε/2.\displaystyle\sup_{\theta\in\Omega_{\varepsilon}}\sup_{s\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\tilde{G}_{n}(s|\theta/\sqrt{n})-\Phi(s|0,\sigma^{2})\Big{|}<\varepsilon/2.

Thus, it follows that

∫−∞∞νn(θ){sups∈ℝ|G~n(s|θ/n)−Φ(s|0,σ2)|}λ(dθ)\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\nu_{n}(\theta)\Big{\{}\sup_{s\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\tilde{G}_{n}(s|\theta/\sqrt{n})-\Phi(s|0,\sigma^{2})\Big{|}\Big{\}}\lambda(d\theta)
=∫Ωενn(θ){sups∈ℝ|G~n(s|θ/n)−Φ(s|0,σ2)|}λ(dθ)+∫Ωε𝖢νn(θ){sups∈ℝ|G~n(s|θ/n)−Φ(s|0,σ2)|}λ(dθ)\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\int_{\Omega_{\varepsilon}}\nu_{n}(\theta)\Big{\{}\sup_{s\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\tilde{G}_{n}(s|\theta/\sqrt{n})-\Phi(s|0,\sigma^{2})\Big{|}\Big{\}}\lambda(d\theta)+\int_{\Omega_{\varepsilon}^{\mathsf{C}}}\nu_{n}(\theta)\Big{\{}\sup_{s\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\tilde{G}_{n}(s|\theta/\sqrt{n})-\Phi(s|0,\sigma^{2})\Big{|}\Big{\}}\lambda(d\theta)
<(ε/2)​∫Ωενn​(θ)​λ​(d​θ)+2​∫Ωε𝖢νn​(θ)​λ​(d​θ)absent𝜀2subscriptsubscriptΩ𝜀subscript𝜈𝑛𝜃𝜆𝑑𝜃2subscriptsuperscriptsubscriptΩ𝜀𝖢subscript𝜈𝑛𝜃𝜆𝑑𝜃\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}<(\varepsilon/2)\int_{\Omega_{\varepsilon}}\nu_{n}(\theta)\lambda(d\theta)+2\int_{\Omega_{\varepsilon}^{\mathsf{C}}}\nu_{n}(\theta)\lambda(d\theta)
<ε.absent𝜀\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}<\varepsilon.

Since ε>0𝜀0\varepsilon>0 is arbitrary,

∫−∞∞νn(θ){sups∈ℝ|G~n(s|θ/n)−Φ(s|0,σ2)|}λ(dθ)=o(1).\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\nu_{n}(\theta)\Big{\{}\sup_{s\in\mathbb{R}}\Big{|}\tilde{G}_{n}(s|\theta/\sqrt{n})-\Phi(s|0,\sigma^{2})\Big{|}\Big{\}}\lambda(d\theta)=o(1).

Therefore, there exists a sequence ςn2→0→subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛0\varsigma^{2}_{n}\to 0 such that

‖qn−νn∗φ0,σ2+ςn2‖∞=o​(1).subscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛∗subscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2subscriptsuperscript𝜍2𝑛𝑜1\displaystyle\|q_{n}-\nu_{n}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}+\varsigma^{2}_{n}}\|_{\infty}=o(1).

Invoking Lemma 2 again finishes the proof. ∎

Proof of Theorem 1.

In view of Lemma 1, it suffices to show that

(μ^π−μπ)2=o​(σπ2)andσ^π2σπ2​→𝑃​1.superscriptsubscript^𝜇𝜋subscript𝜇𝜋2𝑜superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜋2andsuperscriptsubscript^𝜎𝜋2superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜋2𝑃→1\displaystyle(\hat{\mu}_{\pi}-\mu_{\pi})^{2}=o(\sigma_{\pi}^{2})\hskip 20.00003pt\text{and}\hskip 20.00003pt\frac{\hat{\sigma}_{\pi}^{2}}{\sigma_{\pi}^{2}}\overset{P}{\to}1.

Indeed, we have

μ^π−μπsubscript^𝜇𝜋subscript𝜇𝜋\displaystyle\hat{\mu}_{\pi}-\mu_{\pi} =1K​∑k=1K(θk+1m​∑i=1mψ​(Xi,k;θk)+δk)−μπabsent1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾subscript𝜃𝑘1𝑚superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑚𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖𝑘subscript𝜃𝑘subscript𝛿𝑘subscript𝜇𝜋\displaystyle=\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}\Big{(}{\theta}_{k}+\frac{1}{m}\sum_{i=1}^{m}\psi(X_{i,k};{\theta}_{k})+{\delta}_{k}\Big{)}-\mu_{\pi}
=1K​∑k=1K(θk−μπ)+1m​K​∑k=1K∑i=1mψ​(Xi,k;θk)+1K​∑k=1Kδkabsent1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾subscript𝜃𝑘subscript𝜇𝜋1𝑚𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑚𝜓subscript𝑋𝑖𝑘subscript𝜃𝑘1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾subscript𝛿𝑘\displaystyle=\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}({\theta}_{k}-\mu_{\pi})+\frac{1}{mK}\sum_{k=1}^{K}\sum_{i=1}^{m}\psi(X_{i,k};{\theta}_{k})+\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}{\delta}_{k}
=opr​(σπ),absentsubscript𝑜prsubscript𝜎𝜋\displaystyle=o_{\operatorname{pr}}(\sigma_{\pi}),

where the last line is a consequence of Assumption D. Now, for σ^π2superscriptsubscript^𝜎𝜋2\hat{\sigma}_{\pi}^{2}, it follows that

1K​∑k=1K(θ^k−μ^π)2−σ^2m−σπ21𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾superscriptsubscript^𝜃𝑘subscript^𝜇𝜋2superscript^𝜎2𝑚superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜋2\displaystyle\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}(\hat{\theta}_{k}-\hat{\mu}_{\pi})^{2}-\frac{\hat{\sigma}^{2}}{m}-\sigma_{\pi}^{2}
=1K​∑k=1K(θk−μπ)2−σπ2+1K​∑k=1K(θk−θ^k)2−σ2m+2K​∑k=1K(θk−μπ)​(θ^k−θk)−(μπ−μ^π)2+σ2−σ^2mabsent1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝜃𝑘subscript𝜇𝜋2superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜋21𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝜃𝑘subscript^𝜃𝑘2superscript𝜎2𝑚2𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾subscript𝜃𝑘subscript𝜇𝜋subscript^𝜃𝑘subscript𝜃𝑘superscriptsubscript𝜇𝜋subscript^𝜇𝜋2superscript𝜎2superscript^𝜎2𝑚\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}({\theta}_{k}-\mu_{\pi})^{2}-\sigma_{\pi}^{2}+\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}({\theta}_{k}-\hat{\theta}_{k})^{2}-\frac{\sigma^{2}}{m}+\frac{2}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}({\theta}_{k}-\mu_{\pi})(\hat{\theta}_{k}-{\theta}_{k})-(\mu_{\pi}-\hat{\mu}_{\pi})^{2}+\frac{\sigma^{2}-\hat{\sigma}^{2}}{m}

By properties of the Gaussian distribution, we note that

1K​∑k=1K(θk−μπ)2∼σπ2K​χK−12similar-to1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝜃𝑘subscript𝜇𝜋2superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜋2𝐾subscriptsuperscript𝜒2𝐾1\displaystyle\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}({\theta}_{k}-\mu_{\pi})^{2}\sim\frac{\sigma_{\pi}^{2}}{K}\chi^{2}_{K-1}

and so

1K​∑k=1K(θk−μπ)2−σπ2=opr​(σπ2).1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝜃𝑘subscript𝜇𝜋2superscriptsubscript𝜎𝜋2subscript𝑜prsuperscriptsubscript𝜎𝜋2\displaystyle\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}({\theta}_{k}-\mu_{\pi})^{2}-\sigma_{\pi}^{2}=o_{\operatorname{pr}}(\sigma_{\pi}^{2}).

The other terms proceed similarly and are omitted.

For coverage and length, the result follows from combining Propositions 2 and 4 and Corollary 3.1. ∎

Lemma 3.

Suppose Assumptions B and C hold. If an2​bn2→0→superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛20a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2}\to 0, bn2→0→superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛20b_{n}^{2}\to 0, and K​bn→∞→𝐾subscript𝑏𝑛Kb_{n}\to\infty, then

‖πn−π^n‖1=opr​(1).subscriptnormsubscript𝜋𝑛subscript^𝜋𝑛1subscript𝑜pr1\displaystyle\|\pi_{n}-\hat{\pi}_{n}\|_{1}=o_{\operatorname{pr}}(1).
Proof of Lemma 3.

Indeed, consider an auxiliary estimator

q^n​(t)=1K​∑k=1Kφ​(t|an​θ^k,an2​bn2).subscript^𝑞𝑛𝑡1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾𝜑conditional𝑡subscript𝑎𝑛subscript^𝜃𝑘superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2\displaystyle\hat{q}_{n}(t)=\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}\varphi(t|a_{n}\hat{\theta}_{k},a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2}).

Then, a change of variables immediately implies that

‖πn−π^n‖1=‖νn−q^n‖1≤‖νn−ν∞‖1+‖ν∞−q^n‖1.subscriptnormsubscript𝜋𝑛subscript^𝜋𝑛1subscriptnormsubscript𝜈𝑛subscript^𝑞𝑛1subscriptnormsubscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜈1subscriptnormsubscript𝜈subscript^𝑞𝑛1\displaystyle\|\pi_{n}-\hat{\pi}_{n}\|_{1}=\|\nu_{n}-\hat{q}_{n}\|_{1}\leq\|\nu_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\|_{1}+\|\nu_{\infty}-\hat{q}_{n}\|_{1}.

In view of Assumption C and Lemma 2, it suffices to show that

𝔼​‖ν∞−q^n‖22=o​(1).𝔼superscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝜈subscript^𝑞𝑛22𝑜1\displaystyle\mathbb{E}\|\nu_{\infty}-\hat{q}_{n}\|_{2}^{2}=o(1).

Now,

𝔼​‖ν∞−q^n‖22𝔼superscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝜈subscript^𝑞𝑛22\displaystyle\mathbb{E}\|\nu_{\infty}-\hat{q}_{n}\|_{2}^{2} =𝔼​∫−∞∞[ν∞​(x)−q^n​(x)]2​λ​(d​x)absent𝔼superscriptsubscriptsuperscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝜈𝑥subscript^𝑞𝑛𝑥2𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle=\mathbb{E}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}[\nu_{\infty}(x)-\hat{q}_{n}(x)]^{2}\lambda(dx)
=∫−∞∞𝔼​[ν∞​(x)−q^n​(x)]2​λ​(d​x)absentsuperscriptsubscript𝔼superscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝜈𝑥subscript^𝑞𝑛𝑥2𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\mathbb{E}[\nu_{\infty}(x)-\hat{q}_{n}(x)]^{2}\lambda(dx)
=∫−∞∞{𝔼​[ν∞​(x)−q^n​(x)]}2​λ​(d​x)+∫−∞∞Var⁡[q^n​(x)]​λ​(d​x).absentsuperscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝔼delimited-[]subscript𝜈𝑥subscript^𝑞𝑛𝑥2𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsubscriptVarsubscript^𝑞𝑛𝑥𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\{\mathbb{E}[\nu_{\infty}(x)-\hat{q}_{n}(x)]\}^{2}\lambda(dx)+\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\operatorname{Var}[\hat{q}_{n}(x)]\lambda(dx).

For the first term, we have

∫−∞∞{𝔼​[ν∞​(x)−q^n​(x)]}2​λ​(d​x)superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝔼delimited-[]subscript𝜈𝑥subscript^𝑞𝑛𝑥2𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\{\mathbb{E}[\nu_{\infty}(x)-\hat{q}_{n}(x)]\}^{2}\lambda(dx) =∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞[ν∞​(x)−φ​(x|t,an2​bn2)]​qn​(t)​λ​(d​t)}2absentsuperscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝜈𝑥𝜑conditional𝑥𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2subscript𝑞𝑛𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\Big{[}\nu_{\infty}(x)-\varphi(x|t,a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2})\Big{]}q_{n}(t)\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2}
≤2​∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞[ν∞​(x)−φ​(x|t,an2​bn2)]​ν∞​(t)​λ​(d​t)}2absent2superscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝜈𝑥𝜑conditional𝑥𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2subscript𝜈𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle\leq 2\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\Big{[}\nu_{\infty}(x)-\varphi(x|t,a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2})\Big{]}\nu_{\infty}(t)\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2}
+2​∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞φ​(x|t,an2​bn2)​[qn​(t)−ν∞​(t)]​λ​(d​t)}2.2superscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝜑conditional𝑥𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2delimited-[]subscript𝑞𝑛𝑡subscript𝜈𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}+2\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\varphi(x|t,a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2})[q_{n}(t)-\nu_{\infty}(t)]\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2}.

Since an2​bn2→0→superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛20a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2}\to 0, then

∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞[ν∞​(x)−φ​(x|t,an2​bn2)]​ν∞​(t)​λ​(d​t)}2superscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝜈𝑥𝜑conditional𝑥𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2subscript𝜈𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\Big{[}\nu_{\infty}(x)-\varphi(x|t,a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2})\Big{]}\nu_{\infty}(t)\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2} =∫−∞∞[ν∞​(x)−(ν∞∗φ0,an2​bn2)​(x)]2​λ​(d​x)absentsuperscriptsubscriptsuperscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝜈𝑥∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2𝑥2𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}[\nu_{\infty}(x)-(\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2}})(x)]^{2}\lambda(dx)
=‖ν∞−ν∞∗φ0,an2​bn2‖22absentsuperscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝜈∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛222\displaystyle=\|\nu_{\infty}-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2}}\|_{2}^{2}
=o​(1)absent𝑜1\displaystyle=o(1)

by Theorem 4.5.4 of ? (?). Moreover, by Jensen’s inequality, it follows that

∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞φ​(x|t,an2​bn2)​[qn​(t)−ν∞​(t)]​λ​(d​t)}2superscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝜑conditional𝑥𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2delimited-[]subscript𝑞𝑛𝑡subscript𝜈𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\varphi(x|t,a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2})[q_{n}(t)-\nu_{\infty}(t)]\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2} ≤∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​∫−∞∞φ​(x|t,an2​bn2)​[qn​(t)−ν∞​(t)]2​λ​(d​t)absentsuperscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsubscript𝜑conditional𝑥𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2superscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝑞𝑛𝑡subscript𝜈𝑡2𝜆𝑑𝑡\displaystyle\leq\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\varphi(x|t,a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2})[q_{n}(t)-\nu_{\infty}(t)]^{2}\lambda(dt)
=∫−∞∞[qn​(t)−ν∞​(t)]2​λ​(d​t)​∫−∞∞φ​(x|t,an2​bn2)​λ​(d​x)absentsuperscriptsubscriptsuperscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝑞𝑛𝑡subscript𝜈𝑡2𝜆𝑑𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜑conditional𝑥𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑎𝑛2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}[q_{n}(t)-\nu_{\infty}(t)]^{2}\lambda(dt)\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\varphi(x|t,a_{n}^{2}b_{n}^{2})\lambda(dx)
=‖qn−ν∞‖22absentsuperscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛subscript𝜈22\displaystyle=\|q_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\|_{2}^{2}
=o​(1),absent𝑜1\displaystyle=o(1),

where the limit is due to Proposition 4. Finally, note that

∫−∞∞Var⁡[q^n​(x)]​λ​(d​x)=𝒪​(1K​bn)=o​(1)superscriptsubscriptVarsubscript^𝑞𝑛𝑥𝜆𝑑𝑥𝒪1𝐾subscript𝑏𝑛𝑜1\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\operatorname{Var}[\hat{q}_{n}(x)]\lambda(dx)=\mathcal{O}\Big{(}\frac{1}{Kb_{n}}\Big{)}=o(1)

by Proposition 1.4 of ? (?). Combining the above calculations finishes the proof. ∎

Lemma 4.

Suppose Assumptions B, C, and E hold. There exist a sequence {bn}n=1∞superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1\{b_{n}\}_{n=1}^{\infty} with bn​n→0→subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛0b_{n}\sqrt{n}\to 0 such that

‖π~n−πn‖1=opr​(1).subscriptnormsubscript~𝜋𝑛subscript𝜋𝑛1subscript𝑜pr1\displaystyle\|\tilde{\pi}_{n}-\pi_{n}\|_{1}=o_{\operatorname{pr}}(1).
Proof of Lemma 4.

Again, let

ν~n​(x)≜1n​π~n​(x/n)≜subscript~𝜈𝑛𝑥1𝑛subscript~𝜋𝑛𝑥𝑛\displaystyle\tilde{\nu}_{n}(x)\triangleq\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\tilde{\pi}_{n}(x/\sqrt{n}) =12​π​K​n​∑k=1K∫−1/bn1/bnexp⁡(−𝔧​x​z/n)​exp⁡(𝔧​z​θ^k)​exp⁡(σ2​z22​n)​λ​(d​z)absent12𝜋𝐾𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾superscriptsubscript1subscript𝑏𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝔧𝑥𝑧𝑛𝔧𝑧subscript^𝜃𝑘superscript𝜎2superscript𝑧22𝑛𝜆𝑑𝑧\displaystyle=\frac{1}{2\pi K\sqrt{n}}\sum_{k=1}^{K}\int_{-1/b_{n}}^{1/b_{n}}\exp(-\mathfrak{j}xz/\sqrt{n})\exp(\mathfrak{j}z\hat{\theta}_{k})\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}z^{2}}{2n}\Big{)}\lambda(dz)
=12​π​K​∑k=1K∫−1/(bn​n)1/(bn​n)exp⁡(−𝔧​x​z)​exp⁡(𝔧​z​n​θ^k)​exp⁡(σ2​z22)​λ​(d​z).absent12𝜋𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾superscriptsubscript1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛𝔧𝑥𝑧𝔧𝑧𝑛subscript^𝜃𝑘superscript𝜎2superscript𝑧22𝜆𝑑𝑧\displaystyle=\frac{1}{2\pi K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}\int_{-1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n})}^{1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n})}\exp(-\mathfrak{j}xz)\exp(\mathfrak{j}z\sqrt{n}\hat{\theta}_{k})\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}z^{2}}{2}\Big{)}\lambda(dz).

Then,

‖πn−π~n‖1=‖νn−ν~n‖1≤‖νn−ν∞‖1+‖ν∞−ν~n‖1.subscriptnormsubscript𝜋𝑛subscript~𝜋𝑛1subscriptnormsubscript𝜈𝑛subscript~𝜈𝑛1subscriptnormsubscript𝜈𝑛subscript𝜈1subscriptnormsubscript𝜈subscript~𝜈𝑛1\displaystyle\|\pi_{n}-\tilde{\pi}_{n}\|_{1}=\|\nu_{n}-\tilde{\nu}_{n}\|_{1}\leq\|\nu_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\|_{1}+\|\nu_{\infty}-\tilde{\nu}_{n}\|_{1}.

We again show that

𝔼​‖ν∞−ν~n‖22=o​(1).𝔼superscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝜈subscript~𝜈𝑛22𝑜1\displaystyle\mathbb{E}\|\nu_{\infty}-\tilde{\nu}_{n}\|_{2}^{2}=o(1).

By Parseval’s theorem, it follows that

𝔼​‖ν∞−ν~n‖22=𝔼​‖ν∞ft−ν~nft‖22,𝔼superscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝜈subscript~𝜈𝑛22𝔼superscriptsubscriptnormsuperscriptsubscript𝜈ftsuperscriptsubscript~𝜈𝑛ft22\displaystyle\mathbb{E}\|\nu_{\infty}-\tilde{\nu}_{n}\|_{2}^{2}=\mathbb{E}\|\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}-\tilde{\nu}_{n}^{\text{ft}}\|_{2}^{2},

where ν∞ftsuperscriptsubscript𝜈ft\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}} and ν~nftsuperscriptsubscript~𝜈𝑛ft\tilde{\nu}_{n}^{\text{ft}} are the corresponding Fourier transforms of ν∞subscript𝜈\nu_{\infty} and ν~nsubscript~𝜈𝑛\tilde{\nu}_{n}. However, by the Fourier inversion theorem, we have

ν~nft​(x)=𝟙​{x∈(−1/(bn​n),1/(bn​n))}​1K​∑k=1Kexp⁡(𝔧​x​n​θ^k)​exp⁡(σ2​x22).superscriptsubscript~𝜈𝑛ft𝑥1𝑥1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1𝐾superscriptsubscript𝑘1𝐾𝔧𝑥𝑛subscript^𝜃𝑘superscript𝜎2superscript𝑥22\displaystyle\tilde{\nu}_{n}^{\text{ft}}(x)=\mathbbm{1}\left\{x\in(-1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}),1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}))\right\}\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}\exp(\mathfrak{j}x\sqrt{n}\hat{\theta}_{k})\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}x^{2}}{2}\Big{)}.

Recall that

𝔼​‖ν∞ft−ν~nft‖22=∫−∞∞{𝔼​[ν∞ft​(x)−ν~nft​(x)]}2​λ​(d​x)+∫−∞∞Var⁡[ν~nft​(x)]​λ​(d​x).𝔼superscriptsubscriptnormsuperscriptsubscript𝜈ftsuperscriptsubscript~𝜈𝑛ft22superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝔼delimited-[]superscriptsubscript𝜈ft𝑥superscriptsubscript~𝜈𝑛ft𝑥2𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsubscriptVarsuperscriptsubscript~𝜈𝑛ft𝑥𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle\mathbb{E}\|\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}-\tilde{\nu}_{n}^{\text{ft}}\|_{2}^{2}=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\{\mathbb{E}[\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}(x)-\tilde{\nu}_{n}^{\text{ft}}(x)]\}^{2}\lambda(dx)+\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\operatorname{Var}[\tilde{\nu}_{n}^{\text{ft}}(x)]\lambda(dx).

Now,

∫−∞∞𝔼​[ν∞ft​(x)−ν~nft​(x)]2​λ​(d​x)superscriptsubscript𝔼superscriptdelimited-[]superscriptsubscript𝜈ft𝑥superscriptsubscript~𝜈𝑛ft𝑥2𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\mathbb{E}[\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}(x)-\tilde{\nu}_{n}^{\text{ft}}(x)]^{2}\lambda(dx)
=∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞[ν∞ft​(x)−𝟙​{x∈(−1/(bn​n),1/(bn​n))}​exp⁡(𝔧​x​t)​exp⁡(σ2​x22)]​qn​(t)​λ​(d​t)}2absentsuperscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-[]superscriptsubscript𝜈ft𝑥1𝑥1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛𝔧𝑥𝑡superscript𝜎2superscript𝑥22subscript𝑞𝑛𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\Big{[}\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}(x)-\mathbbm{1}\left\{x\in(-1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}),1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}))\right\}\exp(\mathfrak{j}xt)\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}x^{2}}{2}\Big{)}\Big{]}q_{n}(t)\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2}
≤2​∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞[ν∞ft​(x)−𝟙​{x∈(−1/(bn​n),1/(bn​n))}​exp⁡(𝔧​x​t)​exp⁡(σ2​x22)​λ​(d​z)]​(ν∞∗φ0,σ2)​(t)​λ​(d​t)}2absent2superscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-[]superscriptsubscript𝜈ft𝑥1𝑥1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛𝔧𝑥𝑡superscript𝜎2superscript𝑥22𝜆𝑑𝑧∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq 2\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\Big{[}\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}(x)-\mathbbm{1}\left\{x\in(-1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}),1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}))\right\}\exp(\mathfrak{j}xt)\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}x^{2}}{2}\Big{)}\lambda(dz)\Big{]}(\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})(t)\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2}
+2​∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞𝟙​{x∈(−1/(bn​n),1/(bn​n))}​exp⁡(𝔧​x​t)​exp⁡(σ2​x22)​[qn​(t)−(ν∞∗φ0,σ2)​(t)]​λ​(d​t)}2.2superscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscript1𝑥1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛𝔧𝑥𝑡superscript𝜎2superscript𝑥22delimited-[]subscript𝑞𝑛𝑡∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\phantom{\leq}+2\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\mathbbm{1}\left\{x\in(-1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}),1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}))\right\}\exp(\mathfrak{j}xt)\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}x^{2}}{2}\Big{)}[q_{n}(t)-(\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})(t)]\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2}.

For the first term, note that

∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞[ν∞ft​(x)−𝟙​{x∈(−1/(bn​n),1/(bn​n))}​exp⁡(𝔧​x​t)​exp⁡(σ2​x22)​λ​(d​z)]​(ν∞∗φ0,σ2)​(t)​λ​(d​t)}2superscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptdelimited-[]superscriptsubscript𝜈ft𝑥1𝑥1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛𝔧𝑥𝑡superscript𝜎2superscript𝑥22𝜆𝑑𝑧∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\Big{[}\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}(x)-\mathbbm{1}\left\{x\in(-1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}),1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}))\right\}\exp(\mathfrak{j}xt)\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}x^{2}}{2}\Big{)}\lambda(dz)\Big{]}(\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})(t)\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2}
=∫x∉(−1/(bn​n),1/(bn​n))[ν∞ft​(x)]2​λ​(d​x)absentsubscript𝑥1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛superscriptdelimited-[]superscriptsubscript𝜈ft𝑥2𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\int_{x\not\in(-1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}),1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}))}[\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}(x)]^{2}\lambda(dx)
=o​(1)absent𝑜1\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=o(1)

as 1/(bn​n)→∞→1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n})\to\infty by the dominated convergence theorem.

For the other term, we see that

∫−∞∞λ​(d​x)​{∫−∞∞𝟙​{x∈(−1/(bn​n),1/(bn​n))}​exp⁡(𝔧​x​t)​exp⁡(σ2​x22)​[qn​(t)−(ν∞∗φ0,σ2)​(t)]​λ​(d​t)}2superscriptsubscript𝜆𝑑𝑥superscriptsuperscriptsubscript1𝑥1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛𝔧𝑥𝑡superscript𝜎2superscript𝑥22delimited-[]subscript𝑞𝑛𝑡∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎2𝑡𝜆𝑑𝑡2\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\lambda(dx)\Big{\{}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\mathbbm{1}\left\{x\in(-1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}),1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n}))\right\}\exp(\mathfrak{j}xt)\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}x^{2}}{2}\Big{)}[q_{n}(t)-(\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}})(t)]\lambda(dt)\Big{\}}^{2}
=∫−1/(bn​n)1/(bn​n)exp⁡(σ2​x2)​[qnft​(x)−ν∞ft​(x)​φ0,σ2ft​(x)]2​λ​(d​x)absentsuperscriptsubscript1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛superscript𝜎2superscript𝑥2superscriptdelimited-[]superscriptsubscript𝑞𝑛ft𝑥superscriptsubscript𝜈ft𝑥subscriptsuperscript𝜑ft0superscript𝜎2𝑥2𝜆𝑑𝑥\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}=\int_{-1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n})}^{1/(b_{n}\sqrt{n})}\exp(\sigma^{2}x^{2})[q_{n}^{\text{ft}}(x)-\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}(x)\varphi^{\text{ft}}_{0,\sigma^{2}}(x)]^{2}\lambda(dx)
≤exp⁡(σ2bn2​n)​‖qnft−ν∞ft​φ0,σ2ft‖22absentsuperscript𝜎2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2𝑛superscriptsubscriptnormsuperscriptsubscript𝑞𝑛ftsuperscriptsubscript𝜈ftsubscriptsuperscript𝜑ft0superscript𝜎222\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}}{b_{n}^{2}n}\Big{)}\|q_{n}^{\text{ft}}-\nu_{\infty}^{\text{ft}}\varphi^{\text{ft}}_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{2}^{2}
≤exp⁡(σ2bn2​n)​‖qn−ν∞∗φ0,σ2‖22.absentsuperscript𝜎2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑛2𝑛superscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎222\displaystyle\phantom{\leq}\leq\exp\Big{(}\frac{\sigma^{2}}{b_{n}^{2}n}\Big{)}\|q_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{2}^{2}.

Since ‖qn−ν∞∗φ0,σ2‖22=o​(1)superscriptsubscriptnormsubscript𝑞𝑛∗subscript𝜈subscript𝜑0superscript𝜎222𝑜1\|q_{n}-\nu_{\infty}\ast\varphi_{0,\sigma^{2}}\|_{2}^{2}=o(1) by Proposition 4, there exists a sequence bn→0→subscript𝑏𝑛0b_{n}\to 0 with bn​n→0→subscript𝑏𝑛𝑛0b_{n}\sqrt{n}\to 0 such that the above is o​(1)𝑜1o(1).

Finally, applying Proposition 2.1 of ? (?) shows that

∫−∞∞Var⁡[ν~nft​(x)]​λ​(d​x)=o​(1)superscriptsubscriptVarsuperscriptsubscript~𝜈𝑛ft𝑥𝜆𝑑𝑥𝑜1\displaystyle\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\operatorname{Var}[\tilde{\nu}_{n}^{\text{ft}}(x)]\lambda(dx)=o(1)

and combining the above calculations finishes the proof. ∎

Lemma 5.

Consider the setting of Lemma 4. If {κn}n=1∞superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝜅𝑛𝑛1\{\kappa_{n}\}_{n=1}^{\infty} are convex weights with κn→1→subscript𝜅𝑛1\kappa_{n}\to 1, then

‖π^n−πn‖1=opr​(1).subscriptnormsubscript^𝜋𝑛subscript𝜋𝑛1subscript𝑜pr1\displaystyle\|\hat{\pi}_{n}-\pi_{n}\|_{1}=o_{\operatorname{pr}}(1).
Proof of Lemma 5.

Indeed, we have

‖π^n−πn‖1subscriptnormsubscript^𝜋𝑛subscript𝜋𝑛1\displaystyle\|\hat{\pi}_{n}-\pi_{n}\|_{1} =‖κn​max⁡{π~n,0}+(1−κn)​φμ^π,σ^π2−πn‖1absentsubscriptnormsubscript𝜅𝑛subscript~𝜋𝑛01subscript𝜅𝑛subscript𝜑subscript^𝜇𝜋superscriptsubscript^𝜎𝜋2subscript𝜋𝑛1\displaystyle=\|\kappa_{n}\max\{\tilde{\pi}_{n},0\}+(1-\kappa_{n})\varphi_{\hat{\mu}_{\pi},\hat{\sigma}_{\pi}^{2}}-\pi_{n}\|_{1}
≤‖max⁡{π~n,0}−πn‖1+(1−κn)​[‖max⁡{π~n,0}‖1+‖φμ^π,σ^π2‖1].absentsubscriptnormsubscript~𝜋𝑛0subscript𝜋𝑛11subscript𝜅𝑛delimited-[]subscriptnormsubscript~𝜋𝑛01subscriptnormsubscript𝜑subscript^𝜇𝜋superscriptsubscript^𝜎𝜋21\displaystyle\leq\|\max\{\tilde{\pi}_{n},0\}-\pi_{n}\|_{1}+(1-\kappa_{n})\Big{[}\|\max\{\tilde{\pi}_{n},0\}\|_{1}+\|\varphi_{\hat{\mu}_{\pi},\hat{\sigma}_{\pi}^{2}}\|_{1}\Big{]}.

Since πn≥0subscript𝜋𝑛0\pi_{n}\geq 0, it follows that

‖max⁡{π~n,0}−πn‖1≤‖π~n−πn‖1=opr​(1)subscriptnormsubscript~𝜋𝑛0subscript𝜋𝑛1subscriptnormsubscript~𝜋𝑛subscript𝜋𝑛1subscript𝑜pr1\displaystyle\|\max\{\tilde{\pi}_{n},0\}-\pi_{n}\|_{1}\leq\|\tilde{\pi}_{n}-\pi_{n}\|_{1}=o_{\operatorname{pr}}(1)

by Lemma 4. Moreover, ‖max⁡{π~n,0}‖1+‖φμ^π,σ^π2‖1≤2subscriptnormsubscript~𝜋𝑛01subscriptnormsubscript𝜑subscript^𝜇𝜋superscriptsubscript^𝜎𝜋212\|\max\{\tilde{\pi}_{n},0\}\|_{1}+\|\varphi_{\hat{\mu}_{\pi},\hat{\sigma}_{\pi}^{2}}\|_{1}\leq 2, implying

(1−κn)​[‖max⁡{π~n,0}‖1+‖φμ^π,σ^π2‖1]=opr​(1).1subscript𝜅𝑛delimited-[]subscriptnormsubscript~𝜋𝑛01subscriptnormsubscript𝜑subscript^𝜇𝜋superscriptsubscript^𝜎𝜋21subscript𝑜pr1\displaystyle(1-\kappa_{n})\Big{[}\|\max\{\tilde{\pi}_{n},0\}\|_{1}+\|\varphi_{\hat{\mu}_{\pi},\hat{\sigma}_{\pi}^{2}}\|_{1}\Big{]}=o_{\operatorname{pr}}(1).

This finishes the proof. ∎

Proof of Theorem 2.

The proof follows immediately by combining Lemmata 3 and 5 with Proposition 2 and Corollary 3.1. ∎

References

  • Bickel, Klaassen, Ritov & WellnerBickel et al. Bickel, P. J., Klaassen, C. A., Ritov, Y., & Wellner, J. A. (1993). Efficient and adaptive estimation for semiparametric models, volume 4. Springer.
  • BogachevBogachev Bogachev, V. I. (2007). Measure theory, volume 1. Springer.
  • Brown & GreenshteinBrown & Greenshtein Brown, L. D. & Greenshtein, E. (2009). Nonparametric empirical bayes and compound decision approaches to estimation of a high-dimensional vector of normal means. The Annals of Statistics, 37(4), 1685–1704.
  • Chow & TeicherChow & Teicher Chow, Y. S. & Teicher, H. (1997). Probability Theory (3 ed.). Springer.
  • EfronEfron Efron, B. (2014). Two modeling strategies for empirical bayes estimation. Statistical science: a review journal of the Institute of Mathematical Statistics, 29(2), 285.
  • Ignatiadis & WagerIgnatiadis & Wager Ignatiadis, N. & Wager, S. (2022). Confidence intervals for nonparametric empirical bayes analysis. Journal of the American Statistical Association, 117(539), 1149–1166.
  • Javanmard & MontanariJavanmard & Montanari Javanmard, A. & Montanari, A. (2014). Confidence intervals and hypothesis testing for high-dimensional regression. The Journal of Machine Learning Research, 15(1), 2869–2909.
  • Jiang & ZhangJiang & Zhang Jiang, W. & Zhang, C.-H. (2009). General maximum likelihood empirical bayes estimation of normal means. The Annals of Statistics, 37(4), 1647–1684.
  • Martin, Mullis & HooperMartin et al. Martin, M. O., Mullis, I. V., & Hooper, M. (2016). Methods and procedures in TIMSS 2015. TIMSS & PIRLS International Study Center, Lynch School of Education, Boston College and International Association for the Evaluation of Educational Achievement.
  • MeisterMeister Meister, A. (2009). Deconvolution problems in nonparametric statistics (1 ed.). Springer Berlin, Heidelberg.
  • MorrisMorris Morris, C. N. (1983). Parametric empirical bayes inference: theory and applications. Journal of the American statistical Association, 78(381), 47–55.
  • Mullis, Martin & LovelessMullis et al. Mullis, I. V., Martin, M. O., & Loveless, T. (2016). 20 years of TIMSS: International trends in mathematics and science achievement, curriculum, and instruction. TIMSS & PIRLS International Study Center, Lynch School of Education, Boston College and International Association for the Evaluation of Educational Achievement.
  • ProkhorovProkhorov Prokhorov, Y. V. (1952). A local theorem for densities. (russian). Doklady Akad. Nauk SSSR (N.S.), 83, 797–800.
  • Rao & VaradarajanRao & Varadarajan Rao, R. R. & Varadarajan, V. (1960). A limit theorem for densities. Sankhyā: The Indian Journal of Statistics, 22(3/4), 261–266.
  • RobbinsRobbins Robbins, H. (1956). An empirical bayes approach to statistics. Proceedings of the Third Berkeley Symposium on Mathematical Statistics and Probability, 1, 157–163.
  • TsybakovTsybakov Tsybakov, A. B. (2009). Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation (1 ed.). Springer New York.
  • Van de Geer, Bühlmann, Ritov & DezeureVan de Geer et al. Van de Geer, S., Bühlmann, P., Ritov, Y., & Dezeure, R. (2014). On asymptotically optimal confidence regions and tests for high-dimensional models. Annals of Statistics, 42(3), 1166–1202.
  • ZhangZhang Zhang, C.-H. (2003). Compound decision theory and empirical bayes methods. The Annals of Statistics, 31(2), 379–390.
  • Zhang & ZhangZhang & Zhang Zhang, C.-H. & Zhang, S. S. (2014). Confidence intervals for low dimensional parameters in high dimensional linear models. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B: Statistical Methodology, 76(1), 217–242.
